dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

💜Sponsor: Lightdash
🤝Organizer: Jing Yu Lim, Michael Han (Infinite Lambda), Kenneth Li (foodpanda)
🏠Venue Host: foodpanda
🍕Catering: TBD

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

6:30pm - 7:30pm | Food and networking
7:30pm - 8:00pm | Presentation by Hamzah (Founder & CEO of Lightdash, the open source BI platform built natively for dbt)
8:00pm - 8:30pm | Presentation by foodpanda team

🎤 Presentations

#1 The era of the Analytics Intelligence Engineer

Speaker: Hazmah Chaudhary (Lightdash, Founder and CEO)
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/hamzahc
Synopsis: Back in 2018, dbt paved the way for for this new kind of data professional, people who had technical ability and could understand business context. But here’s the thing: AI is automating traditional tasks like pipeline building and dashboard creation. So then what happens to analytics engineers? They don’t disappear - they evolve.The same skills that made analytics engineers valuable also make them perfect for a new role I’m calling ‘Analytics Intelligence Engineers.’ Instead of writing SQL, they’re writing the context that makes AI actually useful for business users.

#2 Handling row deletions when incrementally updating tables

Speaker: Hao Ran Lee (foodpanda, Data Engineer)
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/hao-ran-lee/
Synopsis: Using incremental loads to update a table has various advantages over using a full load (corresponding to the "table" materialization), but complications arise when we have to account for row deletions in the upstream tables. The existing incremental strategies provided by dbt out-of-the-box lack the capability to take care of row deletions in the right way as well. We'll take a look at several ways to handle row deletions from a dbt perspective, going in depth into one approach that my data team has adopted in our daily operations.

#3 Fragile to Agile: dbt Model Versioning to improve change reliability

Speaker: Clarence San (foodpanda, Analytics Engineer)
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/clarencesan/
Synopsis: Changing a core data model often breaks complex downstream dependencies, forcing a choice between innovation and stability. This session dives into model versioning, a practical framework to tackle this dilemma by using versioning principles to your dbt models. We’ll share our use cases, implementation and learnings.

6:30pm - 7:30pm | Food and networking

🎤 Presentations

1) Building and analysing a structured dataset of Singapore's parliamentary speeches - Parleh-Mate

Speaker(s): Jeremy Chia (Linkedin), Royce Hoe (Linkedin)
Synopsis: Parleh-Mate started as a personal project between three friends - an attempt to make Singapore's parliamentary speeches easier to explore and analyse. Along the way, they built a stack with dbt, Python, LLM, and Redner, turning raw transcripts into structured data for analysis. Royce and Jeremy will walk through how the project came together: from data extraction and cleaning, to modelling with dbt, and others. They'll also share lessons learned from running a personal project, and how the small project engaged researchers and the public.

2) Introduction to dbt Fusion

Speaker(s): Jing Yu Lim (Linkedin)
Synopsis: Join us for an exciting peek into dbt Fusion, the new dbt engine announced by dbt Labs. We'll explore the core concepts, understand the rationale driving this major advancement, and witness a demo comparing Fusion's performance against traditional dbt Core.

Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizers: Jing Yu Lim, Michael Han (Infinite Lambda)
🏠Venue Host: Open Sourced (10A Perak Rd)
🍕Catering: TBD, will include vegetarian options

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

🗓️ Agenda:

6:30pm - 7:30pm: Dinner and networking
7:30pm - 8:00pm: Presentation + Q&A by James Nguyen
8:00pm - 8:30pm: Presentation + Q&A by Michael Han
8:30pm - 9:00pm: Networking

🎤 Presentation #1: Medallion vs Data Products: Which one to choose?

Synopsis: In the evolving world of data analytics, structuring, managing, and delivering data in ways that best support business outcomes becomes a great challenge for data gurus. Two prominent approaches in modern data modelling are the Medallion Architecture and Data Products. The presentation evaluates both approaches and mentions “something in between” to facilitate standards and ad-hoc business requirements.
Speaker Bio: James is an experienced data professional with over 4 years of experience in data architecture, modelling and analysis. With expertise in building scalable data pipelines and leveraging modern data frameworks, He has worked with clients operating in different industries to optimise their data strategies and drive actionable business insights.
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nam-nguyen-james-227a64114/

🎤 Presentation #2: Agentic Self-serve: dbt Semantic Layer with Snowflake Cortex Analyst and Streamlit

Synopsis: Discover how to empower non-technical users to independently explore data using dbt's Semantic Layer for metric definitions, Snowflake Cortex Analyst for AI-powered query generation, and Streamlit for intuitive visualization interfaces—creating a powerful, user-friendly data ecosystem that reduces bottlenecks and democratizes data access.
Speaker Bio: Michael has extensive experience in Analytics, Data Science and Engineering, bringing a wealth of knowledge from his work in Singapore's vibrant tech scene. Chat with him about anything (Data, AI or the history of SQL)—he's a fantastic resource for both technical insights and practical applications in fast-evolving fields.
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-ichin-han/

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

With DBT, we have successfully established a sustainable machine learning workflow that empowers us to achieve heightened efficiency, consistency, and explore new frontiers of innovation.

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modelling, testing, and team structures.

🗓️ Date and time: 30th March (Thurs), from 6pm till 9pm
🏠 Venue: Hyper Island Asia
🤝 Organizers: community members Jing Yu Lim, Michael Han
🍕 Catering: TBD

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-singapore channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

🕛 Agenda:

6:00pm - 7:00pm: Registration and networking with food
7:00pm - 7:30pm: 1st presentation with Q&A (Your data is not static: Snapshots and incremental models for change data capture)
7:30pm - 8:00pm: 2nd presentation with Q&A (Implementing a Data Observability Initiative: a practical case with dbt)
8:00pm - 8:45pm: 3rd presentation with Q&A (How would the ideal Semantic Layer look like?)

🎤 Presentation #1 - Your data is not static: Snapshots and incremental models for change data capture

Synopsis: How do we handle analytics reporting use cases that require accurate reporting of point-in-time historical dimensions in the modern data stack? In this talk, Chin Hwee will be exploring how to design efficient and history-preserving data models by adopting change data capture techniques on dbt.
Speaker Bio: Chin Hwee Ong is a senior data engineer and aspiring polymath who happens to have a background in aerospace engineering, computational modelling and business management. As a 90% self-taught programmer with a time-traveling mind, Chin Hwee is currently learning how to design "change-aware" data models in the modern data stack and has a not-so-secret wish of designing a "life versioning system" to keep track of data points in this fast-evolving world.

🎤 Presentation #2 - Implementing a Data Observability Initiative: a practical case with dbt

Synopsis: What is data observability, and what does it accomplish? Michael and Pia will walk through their motivation for implementing a data observability initiative, and some examples of practical implementations. Additionally, they will discuss how we are managing cross-functional processes between data producers (e.g. Engineering, Ops) and consumers (e.g. Data, Product).
Speaker Bio: Michael is the Head of Data at Spenmo, and enjoys gaslighting ChatGPT on his off time. Pia has 12+ years of IT experience with recent applications handling B2B payments processing. As a Lead Data Engineer at Spenmo, she is responsible for designing and implementing data pipelines using dbt and Fivetran for data ingestion.

🎤 Presentation #3 - How would the ideal Semantic Layer look like?

Synopsis: Are you curious about what the ideal semantic layer would look like for your data analytics needs? Join Thanh from Holistics as he explores the characteristics and benefits of the perfect semantic layer.
In this talk, we'll delve into the concept of a semantic layer, why it's become essential in today's analytics landscape, and how it can improve efficiency and user experience. We'll also discuss the essential features that an ideal semantic layer should have and how they can help you achieve your data analysis goals. Whether you're a data analyst, engineer, or business user, this talk will provide valuable insights into the world of semantic layers and how they can transform your approach to data analysis.
Speaker Bio: Thanh currently works at Holistics Software as Co-founder and Chief Engineer architecting the next generation analytics engineering driven BI platform. Before joining Holistics as co-founder, Thanh had 8 years of experience as software engineer and big-data consultant from multiple companies, notably Revolution Analytics which was acquired by Microsoft in 2015.Thanh graduated from National University of Singapore in 2009 majoring in Computer Engineering with a minor in Technopreneurship.

🗓️ Date and time: 7th December (Wednesday), from 6pm till 9pm
🏠 Venue: The Great Room, 18th Floor, Centennial Tower (near Suntec City). Closest MRT - Promenade station.
🤝 Organizers: community members Nas Radev, Jolanda Zwagemaker, Jing Yu Lim, Michael Han
🍕 Catering: to be confirmed

🕛 Agenda:

6:00pm - 6:30pm: Registration and networking with food
6:30pm - 7:00pm: 1st presentation with Q&A (Self-serve: Is it a myth?)
7:15pm - 7:45pm: 2nd presentation with Q&A (Building a data vault using dbt)
8:00pm - 8:30pm: 3rd presentation with Q&A (Get to the point: how to communicate your ideas to busy executives)
8:30pm - 9:00pm: Networking

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-singapore channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

🎤 Presentation #1: Self-serve - Is it a myth?

Description: Data self-serve may be an elusive concept...but we might have figured it out 😉 Gear yourselves up for a sharing from Jing Yu and Michael on how dbt was used for driving self-serve in a startup, along with reflections and learnings.
Speaker Bio: Michael leads the Data Intelligence team in Spenmo, while Jing Yu owns Spenmo's Data Mart as the sole Analytics Engineer. Their greatest nightmare is SQL without leading commas.

🎤 Presentation #2: Building a Data Vault using dbt

Description: Minh will introduce the Data Vault 2.0 data modelling methodology and demonstrate how dbt can be used to create a Data Vault. He will also share some tips and best practices with regards to data quality assurance and dealing with tricky edge cases in data modelling.
Speaker Bio: Hi there! I’m Minh, Analytics Engineer from Infinite Lambda. I’ve been working with DBT for 3 years and with Data Vault for almost 1 year. My current project with Data Vault and DBT focuses on e-commerce with the scale of ~1400 models and 1100 tests. I’d love to share my experiences with the DBT communities and learn more from everyone.

🎤 Presentation #3: Get to the point: how to communicate your ideas to busy executives

Description: We’ve all seen it: The wall of text on Slack that you had to re-read three times to understand. Or the presentation you had to sit through impatiently, waiting for the insight to come. Maybe you wrote that Slack message. I know I have. There’s a MUCH BETTER way to get your points across, be it explaining a dashboard error, presenting an analysis or anything, really. Come listen as I attempt to practise what I preach, and we can ~~argue~~ exchange ideas after. It’ll be fun, I promise.
Speaker Bio: Yuan Ming leads the Analytics team at Toggl, and occasionally has to clean up dbt DAG messes (that he created, mostly). When he’s not data-ing, he enjoys swimming, basketball, cooking and watching videos about… cooking.
